title: Salty Gooey Canadian Sea Bass Gratin
description: This dish combines the richness of sea bass with the savory flavors of caramelized onions and a gooey, cheesy sauce, all while being gluten-free friendly. It's a perfect, cozy meal for a cold Canadian night.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add sliced onions and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 20-25 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a separate saucepan, melt butter over medium heat. Add gluten-free flour and whisk to combine. Cook for 1 minute, forming a roux.
Slowly pour in milk, whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Bring mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens, about 5 minutes.
Remove sauce from heat and stir in cheddar and parmesan cheese until melted and smooth. Season with salt to taste.
Place sea bass fillets in a baking dish. Spoon caramelized onions over the fish, then pour the cheesy sauce over the onions.
Transfer the dish to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the top is golden brown.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
